# REGIME READ — 2026-07-15

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 3 → 3 (unchanged). But for the third read running the direction of travel has flipped — this time back toward relief. The previous read deteriorated hard (its two headline bearish triggers fired); this read shows the first constructive tape since that reversal, though it is fresh, thin, and unconfirmed by the data that matters most.**
- **The single dominant fact of this read: VIX crushed −4.84% to 16.33 (Jul 15), moving decisively AWAY from the 18 bearish trigger.** The prior read's most imminent bearish signal was *"VIX 17.39, 0.61 pts from the 18 bearish trigger — the closest of the sequence."* It has now backed off to **1.67 pts BELOW 18**. The near-trigger did not fire; it reversed.
- **THE BINDING CAVEAT — and it is the same one, unpaid for a second read: VXN is STILL 27.30, still stamped Jul 13, UNCHANGED.** The prior read named its neutralizer precisely: *"The Jul 14 VXN close is the named test."* **That test still has not printed.** VIX has advanced two sessions (to Jul 15) with no corresponding VXN update. **The relief is entirely in the BROAD-market vol gauge; the NASDAQ-specific gauge — the one that actually prices TQQQ — is frozen at its +9.68% spike and its DOWNTURN leg (VXN ≥ 25) remains armed.** For a 3×-Nasdaq instrument, the relief is happening in the wrong index.
- **Brent's third impulse stalled.** 85.13, **+0.47%** (Jul 15) — down from 86.17 / +3.45% in the prior read. **No fourth impulse arrived.** Brent remains 3.13 pts above the 82 trigger (so the trigger stays fired), but the ~15%-in-six-sessions momentum that dominated the last two reads is dead.
- **Gold reversed.** 4,043.70, **−0.43%** (was +0.97%). **The "oil↑ + gold↑ + dollar↓" risk-hedging complex that forced the prior read's geopolitical downgrade has partially dissolved — gold no longer confirms the oil move.** DXY soft but flat (100.98, −0.29%).
- **The authoritative snapshot shows Nasdaq buffers materially LESS stretched than the prior read's chained estimates.** NDX **−3.8%** vs 52W high (the prior read estimated −4.9%); Nasdaq Comp **−4.0%** (prior estimate −4.8%). **The prior read's "bearish first leg 0.2 pts away" was an estimation artifact — on the supplied levels it is ~1.0 pt away.**
- **Total score: the honest number is −2 — and the prior read's number needs correcting.** The prior read's TOTAL row stated **+1**, but its seven lenses (0, 0, −1, −1, −1, 0, +1) actually sum to **−2** — an arithmetic slip. Taking the lenses as authoritative, this read holds every lens and lands at **−2 → −2, unchanged at the lens level.** The improvement is real but it is in DIRECTION and CHARACTER (fresh Jul-15 tape), not yet in the scores — because the fired triggers (VXN >27, Brent >82) have not un-fired, and the relief signals are 1–2 sessions ahead of the stale confirmatory data.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bearish — VIX above 18 sustained:** **BACKED OFF, un-firing.** 16.33 (−4.84%), now **1.67 pts below** (was 0.61 pts below). The closest-ever near-trigger reversed.
  - **Bearish — VXN reverses above 27:** **STILL FIRED, but STALE.** 27.30 (Jul 13, unchanged). Held; no fresh print to confirm or deny.
  - **Bearish — VXN toward 30:** **NOT FIRED, no new data** — 27.30, still 2.70 pts away.
  - **Bearish — Brent fourth impulse / holds above 86:** **NOT FIRED.** 85.13, +0.47% — impulse stalled, slipped back below 86.
  - **Bearish — Nasdaq Comp breaks −5%:** **NOT FIRED — and further away than believed.** −4.0% on the supplied levels, ~1.0 pt from firing (not 0.2).
  - **Bearish — named geopolitical event behind Brent:** **STILL NOT RETRIEVABLE — but the urgency has faded** with Brent flat and gold reversing.
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **STILL NOT REFRESHED. Week 9.**
  - **Bullish — VXN below 25 for 2 sessions:** **STILL DEAD** (27.30). **Bullish — VIX below 15 for 2 sessions:** **NOT FIRED but re-approaching** — 16.33, now 1.33 pts away (narrows from 2.39). **Bullish — Nasdaq Comp buffer above −4%:** **AT the line** (−4.0%, was FAILED at −4.8%). **Bullish — DJI buffer held:** **HOLDS** (−1.5%).
  - **Neutralizer (a fresh VXN close):** **STILL UNPRINTED — now two sessions overdue.** This remains the single unresolved datapoint of the sequence.
- **Notable lens flips: none changed score; three improved in character.** Dealer (VIX relief, offset by stale-high VXN → held 0), Cross-asset (Brent stall + gold reversal → held −1 because Brent is still through 82), Sentiment (VIX crush is the first constructive print → held 0 because equities are stale-down). Geopolitical holds +1. **The read is "less bad, not yet good" — every improvement is real but every one is either fresh-and-unconfirmed or fails the take-the-lower test against a still-fired trigger.**

> **Snapshot mix (widened to two sessions):** Equity indices + **VXN** + 10Y = **Jul 13 Monday close**. **VIX** + DXY + Brent + Gold = **Jul 15 Wednesday intraday**. The timestamp spread is now the story: **the fresh Jul-15 gauges (VIX −4.84%, Brent stall, gold reversal, soft DXY) all point to relief; the stale Jul-13 gauges (VXN spike, all-down equities, yields up) all point to stress.** The relief is newer — but VXN and the equity tape have not updated to confirm it.
>
> **Headline signal #1 — the relief is in the wrong index.** VIX crushed to 16.33; VXN is frozen at 27.30. **Broad-market fear is draining; Nasdaq-specific fear is not — or at least has not been re-measured.** For a 3×-Nasdaq instrument this distinction is decisive: the improvement is happening precisely where the exposure isn't.
>
> **Headline signal #2 — the neutralizer is two sessions overdue.** The prior read made the Jul-14 VXN close its explicit test. VXN has not printed since Jul 13. **The single datapoint that would settle whether the +9.68% spike was a one-session artifact (as the Jul-10 crush turned out to be) or a genuine regime shift is still missing.**
>
> **Headline signal #3 — the oil/gold risk-hedge complex is deflating.** Brent +0.47% (no 4th impulse), gold −0.43% (confirmation withdrawn). **The specific evidence that forced the geopolitical lens down to +1 last read is weakening — but Brent at 85.13 remains above its fired 82 trigger, so the lens stays −1/+1 rather than reverting.**
>
> **Headline signal #4 — yields still rose** (10Y 4.609, +0.88%, Jul 13). Unchanged from the prior read; the equity-down + yields-up discomfort persists in the stale data.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 3 — Neutral/choppy, deterioration pressure easing.** The prior read sat on the Stage-3/Stage-4 boundary with three near-triggers inside 0.61/0.2/2.70 pts. The freshest data has pulled back from that edge: VIX moved 1.67 pts below its trigger, Brent's impulse stalled, and the Nasdaq breadth leg is ~1.0 pt away on authoritative levels. **Stage 3 is now held more comfortably than last read — but it is held on FRESH data that the stale VXN/equity tape has not yet ratified.**
- **Stage Anchor:** Jul 13, 2026 — the VXN reversal (VXN +9.68% to 27.30), still the governing structural event; the Jul-15 relief is a candidate un-anchoring that VXN has not confirmed.
- **Position:** Relief probe, fresh Jul-15 data. **Stage 3 sustain (60%):** VXN prints in the 25–28 band, VIX holds 15–18, Brent stabilizes near 85, equities chop. **Stage 2 recovery (20%):** requires a fresh VXN close back below 25 (from a frozen 27.30) AND VIX holding sub-15 — the VIX half is 1.33 pts away and re-approaching. **Stage 4 re-entry (15%):** a fresh VXN close extends toward 30, or Nasdaq Comp breaks −5%, or a named event surfaces behind oil. **Stage 5 tail (5%):** named geopolitical escalation + HY OAS confirmed wide (unmeasured 9 weeks).
- **Confidence: Medium.** Why Medium: (a) the freshest gauges agree — VIX, Brent, gold and DXY all point the same (relieving) way on Jul 15; (b) the prior read's near-triggers have demonstrably backed off, so the Stage-4 risk is lower than last read. **Not High because:** (1) **the neutralizer is unprinted for a second read — VXN is frozen at its spike and is the exact datapoint that decides the read**; (2) the snapshot spread is now TWO sessions (VIX Jul 15 vs VXN/equities Jul 13), so the relief and the stress are literally measured at different times; (3) **HY OAS unmeasured 9 weeks** — the credit discriminator between "contained tech rotation" and "broad risk-off" is still missing; (4) no breadth internals 9 weeks; (5) **TQQQ close/50DMA broker-unconfirmed for a SIXTH read**; (6) take-the-lower applied at three points, holding scores where character improved.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2):** a **fresh VXN close below 25 for 2 sessions** (frozen at 27.30, unprintable until VXN updates) AND **VIX below 15 for 2 sessions (1.33 pts away, re-approaching)** AND **Nasdaq Comp buffer above −4% (at the line, −4.0%)** AND **Brent back below 82** AND broker-confirmed TQQQ 50DMA reclaim.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** a **fresh VXN close extending toward 30** OR **VIX back above 18** (1.67 pts away, just reversed off it) OR **Nasdaq Comp breaks −5% (~1.0 pt away)** OR **Brent posts a fourth impulse / reclaims 86** OR **a named geopolitical event behind the oil run** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured 9 weeks)**.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **holds Stage 3, with the deterioration pressure of the prior read easing on fresh data but unconfirmed by the gauges that matter — Medium confidence.** The freshest prints all point to relief: **VIX crushed −4.84% to 16.33, reversing 1.67 pts clear of the 18 bearish trigger it was 0.61 pts from firing last read; Brent's third impulse stalled (+0.47%, no fourth impulse); gold reversed (−0.43%), withdrawing its confirmation of the oil move; and on the authoritative supplied levels the Nasdaq breadth leg is ~1.0 pt from firing, not the 0.2 pt the prior read estimated.** **But the relief is entirely in the broad-market gauges (Jul 15) while the Nasdaq-specific and equity data are frozen at Jul 13: VXN is UNCHANGED at its +9.68% spike (27.30), the DOWNTURN vol leg stays armed, and the neutralizer the prior read demanded — a fresh VXN close — is now two sessions overdue.** Brent remains above its fired 82 trigger and the equity session on the board was still all-down, so no lens score changes: **the seven lenses sum to −2 (correcting the prior read's stated +1, which was an arithmetic slip — its lenses summed to −2 as well). Unchanged at the lens level; improving in direction.** **The improvement is real, fresh, and narrow — it has not reached the index TQQQ is levered to.**

Positioning, dealer, sentiment = 0. **Net −2, unchanged at the lens level from the prior read's true sum (its TOTAL row stated +1 in error; its lenses also summed −2).** The direction of travel is constructive but no lens crossed a scoring boundary: every improvement is either fresh-and-unconfirmed (VIX, Sentiment) or blocked by a still-fired trigger (Cross-asset, Breadth). **A fresh VXN close below 25 would upgrade Dealer and likely lift the total; a fresh VXN close toward 30, or a named event, would drop it toward −3.** |

## Diverging Signals
- **THE DOMINANT TENSION — fresh relief vs stale stress, now separated by two full sessions.** Every Jul-15 gauge (VIX −4.84%, Brent stall, gold reversal, soft DXY) says the pressure is releasing; every Jul-13 gauge (VXN +9.68% spike, all-down equities, yields up) says the stress is intact. **The relief is two days newer — but the confirming instruments have not updated.** This is not a contradiction to resolve by judgment; it is a data-timing gap, and the read is explicitly provisional until VXN and the equity tape catch up to VIX.
- **The relief is in the wrong index for this instrument.** VIX (broad) crushed; VXN (Nasdaq) is frozen at its spike. **For a 3×-Nasdaq instrument, broad-market calm that has not reached the Nasdaq vol surface is not protective cover.** The prior read's central finding — Nasdaq-concentrated de-risking — is neither confirmed nor refuted by this read, because the one gauge that would speak to it did not print.
- **The neutralizer is overdue, and this sequence has a documented habit of over-reading single-session VXN moves.** The Jul-10 sub-25 VXN print was a one-session artifact; the Jul-13 +9.68% spike may also prove to be one. **The prior read named the Jul-14 VXN close as the test; it never came, and now the Jul-15 VIX crush tempts an over-read in the opposite (bullish) direction.** The disciplined stance: **do not let two sessions of VIX relief substitute for the missing VXN print.** The spike stands until VXN itself retraces it.
- **The oil story is deflating without ever being named.** Three impulses, ~15%, then a stall and a gold reversal — all with no retrievable catalyst at any point. **If the move was an unnamed supply/geopolitical event, its fade is mildly reassuring; if it was positioning, the fade tells us nothing about what re-ignites it.** Brent above 82 keeps the trigger fired; the lost momentum keeps it from worsening.
- **The credit gap remains the exact unmeasured discriminator.** HY OAS unrefreshed **nine weeks.** The open question — is the (stale) Nasdaq de-risking a contained rotation or the front edge of a broad risk-off — is still answerable only by credit, and credit is still missing. **The VIX relief is consistent with "contained," but VIX is not a credit gauge.**
- **TQQQ's 50DMA is resolved-unfavourable but the resolution is now stale in a friendlier direction.** The est. Jul-13 spot (~$74.86) sits ~2.0% below the $76.39 50DMA — the estimate leans lost. **But the Jul-14/15 sessions (no TQQQ data on the board) coincide with a VIX crush and equity/oil relief; if TQQQ recovered with the tape, the 50DMA question could be closer than the Jul-13 estimate implies.** Broker confirmation — sixth read overdue — is the only way to know.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI estimated **~42–48 — neutral with a mild downward bias, not oversold.** Unchanged; no fresh TQQQ print. The unhelpful middle for a put-seller: no oversold mean-reversion tailwind, and — critically this read — **no confirmation that the broad-market VIX relief has reached the instrument.** Estimate carries six sessions of chained error; do not read to a point.
- **Market structure:** **SIDEWAYS held.** Est. spot −15.0% from the 4-wk high (DOWNTURN needs −20%) and +9.9% above the 4-wk low. **DOWNTURN's vol leg (VXN ≥ 25) remains SATISFIED at 27.30 — but that reading is frozen at Jul 13 and has NOT been re-measured despite two sessions of broad-market relief.** The price leg is still ~5.0 pts (≈ −5.9%) away. **The structural picture is unchanged only because the Nasdaq vol gauge did not update; if VXN retraces with VIX, the DOWNTURN vol leg disarms.** RALLY is 20+ pts away and irrelevant.
- **Trend:** **Est. spot ~2.0% below the 50DMA $76.39 — leans lost.** But note the direction of the estimation uncertainty has friendlier company this read: **the Jul-14/15 sessions (unmeasured for TQQQ) coincided with a VIX crush and equity/oil relief.** If TQQQ tracked that relief, the 50DMA gap is smaller than the Jul-13 estimate. 200DMA cushion ~+9.9%. Both MAs stale (Jun 17). **Broker confirmation of TQQQ's actual recent closes and true 50DMA position remains the highest-priority open item — sixth consecutive read — and now matters more because the tape has moved beneath the last measured point.**
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN 27.30 (Jul 13) — UNCHANGED.** Est. TQQQ IV **~81.9%**, EM **±11.5%** — both frozen. **Premium is still rich on the last reading, but that reading is two sessions stale and the broad-market vol it would normally track (VIX) has since fallen 4.84%.** The structural read: **you cannot tell from this snapshot whether TQQQ premium is still rich or has already crushed with VIX — VXN, the deciding input, was not re-measured.** This is the single largest instrument-level uncertainty in the read. Descriptive only.
- **Put-selling context: CAUTIOUS held — modestly improved from last read, but the improvement is unconfirmed for THIS instrument.** Held CAUTIOUS — not upgraded to FAVOURABLE, not downgraded to HOSTILE — because: (1) **the relief is real but broad (VIX), not Nasdaq-specific (VXN frozen)** — for a 3×-Nasdaq instrument the improvement has not been shown to reach the exposure; (2) **the DOWNTURN vol leg is still armed on the last VXN reading**, even if that reading is stale; (3) **est. spot still ~2.0% below the 50DMA and the one-sigma path still reaches the $68 floor** — the structural picture on the last measured data is unchanged; (4) **the neutralizer (a fresh VXN close) is two sessions overdue**, and this sequence has twice over-read single-session VXN moves; (5) **HY OAS unmeasured nine weeks** — the credit discriminator is still missing. What genuinely improved: **VIX crushed off its trigger, Brent's impulse stalled, gold's risk-hedge confirmation reversed, and the Nasdaq breadth leg is further from firing than the prior read estimated** — the deterioration pressure of the last read has clearly eased. **Net: CAUTIOUS — a less threatening backdrop than the prior read, but one whose improvement lives entirely in fresh broad-market gauges that the stale Nasdaq-specific data (VXN, TQQQ close, equity tape) has not yet confirmed. The three binding items are the overdue fresh VXN close, the sixth-read-overdue broker confirm, and HY OAS. Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.**

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 2):** a **fresh VXN close below 25 for 2 sessions** (frozen at 27.30 — unprintable until VXN updates) AND **VIX below 15 for 2 sessions (1.33 pts away, re-approaching after the crush)** AND **Nasdaq Comp buffer holds above −4% (at the line, −4.0%)** AND **Brent back below 82** AND **broker-confirmed TQQQ 50DMA reclaim.**
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4):** a **fresh VXN close extending toward 30** OR **VIX back above 18 (1.67 pts away, just reversed off it)** OR **Nasdaq Comp breaks −5% (~1.0 pt away)** OR **Brent reclaims 86 / posts a fourth impulse** OR **a named geopolitical event behind the oil run** OR **HY OAS confirmed through 300 bp (unmeasured 9 weeks)**.
